channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Fuel prices fall as Middle East tensions ease @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Fuel prices fall as Middle East tensions ease – herald Addressing delegates at the Annual Chamber of Mines Conference yesterday, Energy and Power Development Minister July Moyo said the Government was actively reviewing pump prices in line with favourable developments on the international market while maintaining its commitment to ensuring uninterrupted fuel supplies. Rutendo [...] The post Fuel prices fall as Middle East tensions ease appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Congolese star now more Zimbabwean @bulawayo24
item.description: Congolese rhumba musician Pitshou Lumiere says he now feels more Zimbabwean than Congolese after spending more than 20 years living and performing in Zimbabwe.The Diamond Musica frontman credited Zimb... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Wicknell Chivayo says King Mswati granted him Eswatini citizenship and diplomatic passport @nehandaradio @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo says he has been granted Eswatini citizenship and a diplomatic passport by King Mswati III following a meeting at the Royal Palace, where they reportedly discussed investment opportunities and regional development. The post Wicknell Chivayo says King Mswati granted him Eswatini citizenship and diplomatic passport appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Scottland FC Raises Player Bonus to US$10,000 Each
item.description: Scottland FC boosts player bonuses to US$10,000 each, signaling ambition, rewarding performance, and energizing the squad ahead. The post Scottland FC Raises Player Bonus to US$10,000 Each appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: The man who killed a dead lion: Reason Wafawarova takes aim at Rutendo Matinyarare @nehandaradio
item.description: In a provocative opinion piece, Reason Wafawarova questions Rutendo Matinyarare’s claim that he was instrumental in removing sanctions, arguing that geopolitical realities had already rendered the policy unsustainable. The post The man who killed a dead lion: Reason Wafawarova takes aim at Rutendo Matinyarare appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe’s future cannot be built on tanks: The danger of another military intervention @nehandaradio
item.description: As calls for another military intervention grow louder in some political circles, Gabriel Manyati argues that Zimbabwe’s future lies in stronger democratic institutions, not tanks on the streets. The post Zimbabwe’s future cannot be built on tanks: The danger of another military intervention appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Full Transcript: Reply To The Second Reading Debate of Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill, 2026 By Hon. Ziyambi Ziyambi @263Chat
item.description: Introduction Mr. Speaker Sir, I rise to reply to the Second Reading debate on the Constitution of Zimbabwe (Amendment No.3) H.B.1. Bill, 2026. Before doing so, may I thank Honourable Members for their spirited and constructive engagement of the Bill. Over seven sitting days, members [...] The post Full Transcript: Reply To The Second Reading Debate of Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill, 2026 By Hon. Ziyambi Ziyambi appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: President Mnangagwa’s faction Risks Violent Coup... Military Faction Warns of Mugabe’s Fate as Generals Turn Against ED
item.description: The current political climate in Zimbabwe is fraught with tension, as President Emmerson Mnangagwa, once hailed as a pragmatist after the 2017 coup that unseated Robert Mugabe, now faces a formidable challenge from within his own ruling establishment. His ambition to extend his tenure beyond the constitutionally mandated term has ignited a fierce internal struggle, [...] The post President Mnangagwa’s faction Risks Violent Coup... Military Faction Warns of Mugabe’s Fate as Generals Turn Against ED first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
